description: In Python, run the package-local validation gate (the package's editable install (pip install -e / uv sync) inside its venv) and summarize the diagnostics.
body: In Python, this capability runs the owning package's gate and reports the outcome so the next action is obvious. Use when you need an authoritative pass/fail for a Python package before or after a change. Do not run a root or generic command that bypasses the package's real config, and avoid reporting success without the exit code. Actions: take the package path, run import-time evaluation plus python -m py_compile on changed modules then mypy/pyright type checks, ruff/flake8 lint, and DeprecationWarnings and pytest on the changed package inside the project venv, and record the command, exit code, the last frame of the traceback owned by your code, and the recommended next step. Validation signals include the captured command log, the exit code, and the first diagnostics with file and line. Risk boundary: surface, do not auto-fix; escalate when the gate requires network access, credentials, or a toolchain change.
